steps.title: Identify the given information and set up the proportion
steps.content: The provider surveyed a sample of 400 customers from a total population of 48,000 customers. In the sample, 7 customers said they would be interested in the new plan. To estimate the total number interested in the full population, we use the sample proportion to scale up.

steps.title: Scale the sample to estimate the population
steps.content:
Find the scaling factor from sample to population:
$$\frac{48,000}{400} = 120$$
The population is 120 times the sample size, so multiply the number of interested customers in the sample by 120:
$$7 \times 120 = 840$$
C is correct.
